name: Web Realtime
description: Standard for the open-app realtime transport — the live, bidirectional-or-streamed server→client delivery channel an open app holds (presence, live data, collaborative cursors, server-pushed updates). Defines the transport-negotiation Protocol: a CustomTransportProvider seam that auto-negotiates the best available channel — SSE and WebSocket native-first, WebTransport capability-gated (Chrome/Edge only, no Safari — graceful degradation), long-poll/poll as the degradation floor — so a consuming app declares realtime delivery and the provider picks the transport, the way Socket.IO / SignalR / Centrifugo auto-negotiate. Ratified in #455 (Fork 2, transport home by purpose). Deliberately separated from Web Notifications’ closed-app push: the two are non-fungible — a live socket and a service-worker push survive opposite lifecycles (open tab vs. closed app) and you never fall back from one to the other. Technical contract, not a UX preference — a Protocol, not an Intent.
